text:
In a quaint little town whose name fell somewhere between sleepy and sleepytown, lived a boy named Oliver. Oliver had eyes like the deepest ocean, and his imagination? Well, it was an ocean of its own.

Every morning, before anything else crossed his mind, Oliver would scamper across the dew-kissed lawns to his wooden rocking chair by the front window. The chair stood as a modest sentinel to his little world—a world where bears were best friends with kittens, dragons wore tiny hats, and time could be rewound like a record.

One crisp autumn day, the leaves around town were a riotous blend of fiery reds, soft oranges, and a few hesitant greens. Oliver’s room had been transformed into a winter wonderland. His bed, a mountain of blankets, was adorned with reindeer antlers and a tiny frost crystal as a hat. On the shelves by his bed, a winter scene painting had been placed with such reverence that one could swear Santa Claus had personally
